name: Grilled Asian Chicken
instructions:
1. Gather and measure all your ingredients before starting to ensure a smooth cooking process.

2. In a small microwave-safe bowl, combine the soy sauce, honey, sesame oil, minced garlic, and grated ginger. Heat in the microwave on medium power for 1 minute, then stir to blend the flavors. Heat again for 30 seconds, watching closely to prevent the mixture from boiling over.

3. Place the chicken breasts in a shallow dish or resealable bag. Pour the warm soy sauce mixture over the chicken, turning to coat all sides. Set aside to marinate at room temperature for 15 minutes, allowing the flavors to penetrate the meat.

4. Preheat your outdoor grill to medium-high heat. Lightly oil the grate using a paper towel dipped in oil to prevent the chicken from sticking.

5. Remove the chicken from the marinade, allowing excess to drip off. Transfer the remaining marinade to a small saucepan and bring to a rolling boil over medium-high heat. Reduce the heat to low and simmer until the marinade thickens slightly, about 1 minute. Set aside for basting during grilling.

6. Place the chicken on the preheated grill and cook, basting frequently with the reserved thickened marinade, until the chicken develops beautiful golden-brown grill marks on all sides and is no longer pink in the center, about 6 to 8 minutes per side.

7. To ensure doneness, insert an instant-read meat thermometer into the thickest part of the chicken—it should read at least 165°F (74°C). Remove from grill, let rest for a few minutes, and serve.
simpleInstructions:
1. Mix soy sauce, honey, sesame oil, garlic, and ginger, then microwave until warm.

2. Marinate chicken breasts for 15 minutes in the mixture.

3. Boil remaining marinade until thickened for basting.

4. Grill chicken 6-8 minutes per side, basting often, until golden and cooked through to 165°F!
